alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google Devices and Services with two promotions, scaling global commercial operations and leading transformation programs, suggesting multi-year track record of owning and leading strategic initiatives
alignments.job_requirement: Scope and lead technical initiatives end-to-end: identify problems worth solving, propose the right solution approach, and deliver on that solution

alignments.candidate_evidence: Experience scaling global commercial operations indicates ability to juggle multiple workstreams and priorities in complex, fast-paced environments
alignments.job_requirement: Manage multiple projects simultaneously and prioritize tasks effectively in a fast-paced setting

alignments.candidate_evidence: Strategy and Operations role typically requires cross-functional stakeholder management and communication across technical and business teams
alignments.job_requirement: Excellent written and verbal communication across technical and non-technical audiences

gaps.gap: No software engineering or production code background; profile explicitly states no production software-engineering background and Python only basic/actively learning
gaps.mitigation: Role requires writing high-quality production code at scale, reading/writing code, SQL expertise, and technical debugging. While strong operational thinking can accelerate technical learning, the code-writing and SQL-heavy nature of the role and three-year minimum technical role requirement are core to the job, not nice-to-have

gaps.gap: No demonstrated SQL or data pipeline expertise; candidate background is in operations and transformation, not data engineering or analytics
gaps.mitigation: JD requires 'Expertise in SQL and data visualization, proven experience analyzing complex datasets' as a minimum requirement. No evidence of this in profile

gaps.gap: No financial services, accounting, compliance, or payments domain experience mentioned; Google Devices is hardware/consumer tech, not fintech
gaps.mitigation: Preferred qualification, but understanding financial data quality, anomaly detection, and regulatory context is core to TechOps role. Candidate would require significant domain ramp

gaps.gap: No demonstrated experience in Technical Operations, Technical Support, Solutions Architecture, or Integration Engineering roles; background is pure Strategy/Operations
gaps.mitigation: JD explicitly requires 3+ years in 'a technical role (e.g., Technical Operations, Software Engineer, etc.) or a technical user facing role (e.g., Solutions Architecture, Integration Engineer).' Candidate does not meet this minimum requirement

hard_blockers.evidence: Minimum requirement: 'Have 3+ years professional experience in a technical role (eg: Technical Operations, Software Engineer, etc.) or a technical user facing role (e.g. Solutions Architecture, Integration Engineer).' Candidate's background is Strategy and Operations at Google, with no technical role experience, software engineering, or solutions architecture documented.

Minimum requirement: 'Write high-quality code that scales effectively.' Candidate profile explicitly states 'No production software-engineering background' and 'Python basic and actively learning,' failing the core code-writing requirement.

Minimum requirement: 'Expertise in SQL and data visualization, proven experience analyzing complex datasets.' No evidence of SQL expertise, data pipeline analysis, or data visualization experience in candidate profile.

output.summary: Integration Reliability Engineer is a technical operations and payments engineering role requiring 3+ years hands-on technical experience (code, SQL, debugging systems), high-quality production code, and SQL/data expertise. Candidate is a Strategy and Operations leader with Google scaling and transformation experience but no software engineering, SQL, technical operations, or fintech background. Misalignment is fundamental and disqualifying on three separate minimum requirements.
uncertainties: Whether candidate has any undocumented SQL, data engineering, or technical tooling experience from operations or transformation work at Google
Degree of technical depth in 'systems work' during Devices/Services transformation programs—whether this included code-level debugging or purely architectural oversight
Candidate's current pace and depth of Python learning; whether this could accelerate to 'high-quality production code' standard within onboarding
Whether 'financial data quality' exposure in commercial operations scaling translates to payments/fintech domain knowledge
